text: Generative models have been a paradigm shift in the field of artificial intelligence (AI) in the recent years. Many generative models have shown high-level performance for a wide range of supervised and unsupervised learning problems. Using generative models have shown great promises in many real-world applications, such as medical image analysis, image-to-image translation, language modeling and multimodal learning, etc. This paper provides a survey of the state-of-the-art research works on Generative AI and delves into the new architectures and models proposed for specific applications. It also gives a comprehensive analysis on the impact of large language models for text generation and the success of generative models in producing highly realistic results for both visual and multi-modal data. This paper is one of the pioneering works that not only provides a comprehensive survey of the various generative models such as Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Variational Autoencoders (VAEs), Transformers and Diffusion models but also shows the integration of the recent advancements in this field and also gives an outlook of the future research works in the Generative AI. In addition, this paper also highlights the necessity of the “Responsible AI” and also lists the ethical and sustainable aspects that should be considered for the design, implementation and usage of generative AI models.
headline: Fully Human Written
